sections.paragraph: (“dwelling,” from חֶדֶר and ךְ formative, as in גַּנְזַךְ), [Hadrach], pr.n. of a city and a region of the same name, situated to the east of Damascus; it occurs once Zec 9:1. There are not any certain traces of this place, for the trustworthiness of R. Jose of Damascus, and of Joseph Abassi, may well be called in question; see Jo. D. Michaëlis Suppl. p. 676. Also see Van Alphen, De Terra Hadrach et Damasco, Traj. 1723, 8; and in Ugolini Thes.t. vii. No. 20.
